codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation that adds proactive token refresh to the Copilot Check Status button. The OAuthStatusRefresher interface is a good optional-interface pattern. The handler gracefully degrades when refresh fails. Tests cover success, missing tokens, unknown backends, and graceful failure paths.

issues.description: The OAuthCheckStatus handler always returns the full OAuth status fragment for ALL backends (via u.registry.OAuthStatuses()), not just the one that was checked. This is functionally correct since the template renders all backends and HTMX replaces the entire container, but it means a check-status request for one backend also queries status for all others. If the number of OAuth backends grows, this could become inefficient. Not blocking because the current scale is small (2-3 backends) and the approach ensures consistency.

RefreshOAuthStatus delegates entirely to getCopilotToken(), which re-exchanges the GitHub token when the Copilot token is expired. If the token is still valid (not expired), getCopilotToken returns the cached token without any network call, so the 'Check Status' button becomes a no-op for valid tokens. This is technically correct but may be surprising — the button says 'Check Status' implying it will verify the token still works with the upstream API, but for valid tokens it just returns the cached state. This matches the original behavior for valid tokens but is worth noting.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The new OAuthStatusRefresher optional interface in backend.go is not documented in .factory/library/architecture.md or .factory/library/copilot-backend.md. The architecture docs describe the backend interfaces (Backend, OAuthStatusProvider, OAuthLoginHandler, OAuthDeviceCodeLoginHandler) but not this new one. Future workers implementing backends with proactive refresh capability won't know about this interface without reading the code.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md lists interfaces in Backend Registry section; .factory/library/copilot-backend.md describes token lifecycle but not RefreshOAuthStatus

sharedStateObservations.observation: The project has no CSRF protection on any POST endpoints (observed by checking all r.Post() routes in main.go). This is an existing pattern, not introduced by this feature. All POST routes including /config/save, /oauth/disconnect, and the new /oauth/check-status lack CSRF tokens. This is consistent but worth noting for security awareness.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: cmd/llmapiproxy/main.go lines 69-100 — all Post routes have no CSRF middleware

summary: The implementation correctly fixes the Copilot Check Status button to proactively trigger token discovery and exchange. The OAuthStatusRefresher optional interface is a clean extension point. The handler gracefully handles refresh failures by still rendering the current status. All 6 new tests pass, covering the success case, error cases (no token, no GitHub token, unknown backend), and graceful degradation. Two minor non-blocking observations: the handler refreshes all backend statuses (not just the requested one), and for valid unexpired tokens the refresh is effectively a no-op (reads cached state). The implementation fully satisfies the feature description and expected behavior requirements.
